messages.content:
Answer "A representative has proposed a bill on criminal justice reform. What kind of rule would she likely prefer and why?

Group of answer choices

closed rule, because she does not want amendments offered that could potentially kill or water down the bill

closed rule, because this would eliminate the possibility of the president vetoing the bill

open rule, because most legislators want others to offer amendments to their legislation to make it better

open rule because this would mean it could bypass the Senate and immediately go to the president for signature"

A representative who has proposed a bill on criminal justice reform and wants to prevent the possibility of amendments that could potentially derail or weaken the bill would likely prefer a "closed rule." This preference arises because a closed rule restricts or completely prohibits the introduction of amendments during the floor debate. This control over amendments can help ensure that the bill remains intact as per the representative's vision and objectives, reducing the risk of modifications that could compromise its original intent or effectiveness.

So, the correct choice is:

"closed rule, because she does not want amendments offered that could potentially kill or water down the bill."

Answer "One advantage of seniority in Congress is it makes a congressperson

Group of answer choices

less likely to participate in committee work.   

more likely to get leadership in a committee.

less likely to have to participate in subcommittees.

less likely to have to serve as president pro tempore."

One advantage of seniority in Congress is it makes a congressperson

"more likely to get leadership in a committee."

Seniority is often a major factor in determining committee leadership roles within Congress. Members with longer tenure are typically given priority when leadership positions, such as committee chairmanships, are assigned. This system rewards experience and longevity in office, under the presumption that senior members have accumulated the knowledge, expertise, and relationships necessary to effectively lead and navigate legislative processes.

Answer "Why might ping-ponging procedures be preferred over calling a conference committee to reconcile Senate and House differences?

Group of answer choices

An opponent has more opportunities to block a bill during a conference committee by adding unfavorable amendments or by using the filibuster but party leaders can shield much of this through ping-ponging.

Ping-ponging is more deliberative and gives many more individuals a say in the bill’s contents so legislation can improve through this process compared to conference committees.

Party leaders have more control over conference committees because they appoint them, but ping-ponging allows minority party members more input into the final bill.

The president controls the conference committee, while members of Congress can have more control over ping-ponging."

The correct choice is:

"An opponent has more opportunities to block a bill during a conference committee by adding unfavorable amendments or by using the filibuster but party leaders can shield much of this through ping-ponging."

In the process of ping-ponging, a bill is sent back and forth between the House and the Senate until both chambers agree on the text. This method can be preferred because it allows the leadership to more directly manage the negotiation process and potentially bypass or limit the opportunities for opponents to derail the legislation with amendments or filibusters that are more likely to occur during a conference committee. Conference committees involve a selected group of members from both chambers working out the differences, and the process can be more formal and susceptible to delays or obstructions. In contrast, ping-ponging keeps the bill within the more controlled environments of each chamber's floor proceedings, under the closer oversight of party leadership.

Answer "Oversight can best be described as the

Group of answer choices

efforts of Congress to supervise the manner in which its laws are implemented by the executive branch.

organizational control exercised by party whips over members of Congress.

authority of committee chairs over the hearings and investigations conducted by the subcommittees.

approval that the Senate must give to all presidential appointments to the federal judiciary."

Oversight can best be described as the

"efforts of Congress to supervise the manner in which its laws are implemented by the executive branch."

Oversight refers to the review, monitoring, and supervision of federal agencies, programs, activities, and policy implementation by the executive branch. Congress exercises this power to ensure laws are administered in accordance with legislative intent, to prevent waste and fraud, to protect civil liberties and rights, and to secure the effective operation of government.

Answer "The apportionment of voters in districts in such a way as to give unfair advantage to one racial or ethnic group or political party is known as

Group of answer choices

the census.

elections.

gerrymandering.

redistricting."

The apportionment of voters in districts in such a way as to give unfair advantage to one racial or ethnic group or political party is known as

"gerrymandering."

Answer "A major risk of the delegate model of representation is that

Group of answer choices

no new laws will be passed because all representatives must agree before a bill can be enacted.

elected officials will spend too much time attempting to raise money for their next campaign because they must seek re-election so frequently.

elected officials will completely ignore their constituents’ preferences because they are following their own beliefs about which policies are best.

the voices of only a few active and informed constituents will be heard because most people do not pay close attention to every issue."

A major risk of the delegate model of representation is that

"the voices of only a few active and informed constituents will be heard because most people do not pay close attention to every issue."

In the delegate model of representation, elected officials seek to act directly in accordance with the wishes and preferences of their constituents, essentially serving as their delegates. A significant risk of this model is that it might disproportionately amplify the views of a vocal minority who are more politically engaged, while the broader preferences of the less active or informed majority of constituents might not be adequately represented. This can lead to policy decisions that do not accurately reflect the wants or needs of the entire community or constituency.

Answer "A major risk of the delegate model of representation is that

Group of answer choices

no new laws will be passed because all representatives must agree before a bill can be enacted.

elected officials will spend too much time attempting to raise money for their next campaign because they must seek re-election so frequently.

elected officials will completely ignore their constituents’ preferences because they are following their own beliefs about which policies are best.

the voices of only a few active and informed constituents will be heard because most people do not pay close attention to every issue."

A major risk of the delegate model of representation is that

"the voices of only a few active and informed constituents will be heard because most people do not pay close attention to every issue."

This risk implies that in attempting to directly represent the wishes of their constituents, elected officials might end up disproportionately catering to those who are most vocal or engaged, potentially neglecting the broader interests of the entire electorate, many of whom may not be as active or informed about every specific issue.

Answer "When Democratic members of Congress were considering the Affordable Care Act, many were divided on the bill. Some favored it and others opposed it. What would be the role of the whip in this situation?

Group of answer choices

to make committee assignments

to call the gathering of a conference

to line up party members on a vote for this bill

to elect the minority party leader"

In the situation where Democratic members of Congress were divided on the Affordable Care Act, the role of the whip would be

"to line up party members on a vote for this bill."

The primary function of the whip in any legislative body is to secure party discipline, especially on key votes. In this context, the Democratic whip would work to ensure that members of their party are aligned in their voting, aiming to marshal the necessary support for the Affordable Care Act to pass. This involves counting votes, persuading undecided or wavering legislators, and coordinating the party's legislative strategy to achieve a favorable outcome.

Answer "Which of the following statements about the role of constituents in congressional decision making is true?

Group of answer choices

Constituents play an important role in members of Congress’s decisions since members would like to be reelected.

Since constituents pay little attention to politics, members of Congress do not consider their opinions when making decisions.

Most members of Congress win reelection so they do not have to consider constituents’ opinion as much as other factors like what their party or special interest groups want.

Over time, as party polarization has increased, there has been less need for members to consider constituents’ opinions because voters just rely on partisan cues when voting."

The statement about the role of constituents in congressional decision making that is true is:

"Constituents play an important role in members of Congress’s decisions since members would like to be reelected."

Members of Congress are keenly aware of the preferences and opinions of their constituents because their primary goal often includes being reelected. They engage in various activities such as holding town hall meetings, sending newsletters, and maintaining active social media channels to gauge public opinion in their districts or states. The desire for reelection motivates them to align their legislative actions with the interests and preferences of their constituents to some extent, as alienating voters could jeopardize their chances in the next election.

Answer "Which of the following statements about partisan polarization in Congress is true?

Group of answer choices

Republicans and Democrats in Congress are ideologically very similar, which has led some progressives and conservatives to propose working together to establish a third party.

Republicans and Democrats in Congress used to be very polarized in the 1960s and 1970s, but the parties have increasingly worked together to pass large-scale legislative proposals.

Republicans and Democrats in Congress have been diverging since the mid-1970s and are more polarized now than at any time in the last century.

In the 1990s, Democrats and Republicans in Congress began diverging ideologically, yet the trend has reversed with the election of Joe Biden as president."

The statement about partisan polarization in Congress that is true is:

"Republicans and Democrats in Congress have been diverging since the mid-1970s and are more polarized now than at any time in the last century."

Partisan polarization refers to the degree of ideological division and opposition between political parties. Over the past several decades, the gap between Republicans and Democrats in Congress concerning ideological and policy positions has markedly increased. This divergence has led to heightened partisanship, with both parties showing less willingness to compromise, which has significant implications for the legislative process and governance.

Answer "The growth of the Latino population in the United States is significant because

Group of answer choices

states with large Latino populations would lose seats in Congress.

this would not change the apportionment that happens every 10 years.

Latino voters would be less likely to be represented in Congress.

states with sizeable Latino populations could gain seats in Congress."

The growth of the Latino population in the United States is significant because

"states with sizeable Latino populations could gain seats in Congress."

The apportionment of seats in the United States House of Representatives is based on population, as determined by the decennial census. As the Latino population in the United States grows, states with large Latino populations have the potential to gain additional seats in Congress due to this increase in population. This demographic shift can influence political representation and potentially shift political power within Congress.

Answer "How do the different constituencies represented by U.S. representatives and senators shape how these members perform their duties?

Group of answer choices

Representatives generally only focus on pork barrel efforts due to their narrow constituency, while senators never engage in pork barrel politics because it may alienate constituents who do not receive the benefits.

Since representatives serve small constituencies, they do not rely on special interest groups; senators are much more reliant on campaign donations and therefore less in tune with voters of their state.

Representatives serve a small constituency and therefore must focus on constituent service and casework, while senators serve a broad, statewide constituency and therefore do not engage in casework or constituent service.

Due to a small, more uniform constituency and frequent elections, representatives are often more attuned to narrower, local interests, while senators, with their statewide, diverse consistency, are a bit more insulated and must balance competing influences."

"Due to a small, more uniform constituency and frequent elections, representatives are often more attuned to narrower, local interests, while senators, with their statewide, diverse consistency, are a bit more insulated and must balance competing influences."

U.S. Representatives serve smaller districts within states and are elected every two years, making them more sensitive to the local and immediate concerns of their constituents. This often necessitates a focus on casework, constituent services, and local issues. Senators, on the other hand, represent entire states and are elected every six years. This broader constituency includes a more diverse set of interests, requiring senators to balance and address the needs and concerns of various groups within their state. While senators also engage in constituent services and casework, the scope and nature of the issues they address can be broader compared to that of representatives.

answer "A major risk of the delegate model of representation is that

  no new laws will be passed because all representatives must agree before a bill can be enacted.
  elected officials will spend too much time attempting to raise money for their next campaign because they must seek re-election so frequently.
  elected officials will completely ignore their constituents’ preferences because they are following their own beliefs about which policies are best.
  the voices of only a few active and informed constituents will be heard because most people do not pay close attention to every issue."

A major risk of the delegate model of representation is that

"the voices of only a few active and informed constituents will be heard because most people do not pay close attention to every issue."

Answer "What is the role of the Office of Management and Budget?

Group of answer choices

It can veto legislation passed by Congress that negatively impacts the federal budget.

It grants approval to the president when they seek administrative rule changes and tax increases.

It passes the federal budget.

It assists with overseeing regulatory proposals and preparing the national budget."

The role of the Office of Management and Budget is

"It assists with overseeing regulatory proposals and preparing the national budget."

answer "Which of the following describes the claim that confidential communications between a president and close advisers should not be revealed without the consent of the president?

Group of answer choices

executive agreement

executive consent

executive privilege

executive order"

The claim that confidential communications between a president and close advisers should not be revealed without the consent of the president is described as

"executive privilege."

answer "Presidents have expanded their powers beyond what they possessed in the 1800s and early 1900s in two major ways. The first has been to directly develop popular support for their agenda, called going public. The other has been by

Group of answer choices

buying off interest groups with offers of jobs in the administration.

making direct appeals to the state governors and legislatures to try and enact their proposals.

diminishing the authority of the judicial branch, especially by refusing to administer their decisions.

reducing their dependence on Congress by giving themselves independent governing capabilities.
"

The other major way presidents have expanded their powers beyond what they possessed in the 1800s and early 1900s, alongside directly developing popular support for their agenda (going public), has been by

"reducing their dependence on Congress by giving themselves independent governing capabilities."

answer "When the president grants a pardon to someone convicted of a federal crime, this is an example of using one of their ________ powers.

Group of answer choices

expressed

delegated

inherent

implied"
When the president grants a pardon to someone convicted of a federal crime, this is an example of using one of their **expressed** powers.

answer only "What is the main difference between inherent and implied powers?

Group of answer choices

Inherent powers can be traced to the powers expressed in the actual language of the Constitution, while implied powers derive from congressional laws and Supreme Court decisions.

Implied powers can be traced to the powers expressed in the actual language of the Constitution, while inherent powers derive from congressional laws and Supreme Court decisions.

Implied powers can be traced to the powers expressed in the actual language of the Constitution, while inherent powers derive from national sovereignty.

Inherent powers can be traced to the powers expressed in the actual language of the Constitution, while implied powers derive from national sovereignty."
Implied powers can be traced to the powers expressed in the actual language of the Constitution, while inherent powers derive from national sovereignty.

answer only "In a striking example of exerting inherent powers, which president cited their oath of office as justification for suspending the writ of habeas corpus?

Group of answer choices

Thomas Jefferson

Abraham Lincoln

Franklin Delano Roosevelt

Bill Clinton"
Abraham Lincoln

answer only "The main political value of the vice president is to

Group of answer choices

bring the president votes in the election that he or she might otherwise not win.

draw negative attention away from the president during times of crisis.

give the president an institutional link to Congress.

promote bipartisanship with members of the opposing political party through negotiations."
bring the president votes in the election that he or she might otherwise not win.

answer only "The president’s military powers are most expressed in the role of

Group of answer choices

the head of their party.

the head of state.

the commander in chief.

the only official elected by the entire country."
the commander in chief.

answer only "Executive agreements differ from formal treaties in that they

Group of answer choices

must be approved by the U.S. Supreme Court but not by Congress.

do not have to be approved by two-thirds vote of the Senate.

do not have to be approved by two-thirds vote of the House.

are generally formulated at meetings of the United Nations."
do not have to be approved by two-thirds vote of the Senate.

answer only "Who among the following is a member of the National Security Council?

Group of answer choices

the Chief Justice

the secretary of defense

the chairperson of the Foreign Relations Committee in the Senate

any living former president"
the secretary of defense

answer only "There are ________ federal government departments and each is led by a ________ who is a member of the president’s cabinet.

Group of answer choices

2; secretary

5; senator

15; secretary

50; senator"
15; secretary

answer only "Which of these statements about Vice Presidents is true?

Group of answer choices

Only once has a vice president had to replace a president in office.

Vice presidents are not empowered to replace a president in office unless confirmed by the Senate.

Several vice presidents have had to take on the presidency after the president has died or resigned.

The vice president is behind the Chief Justice of the Supreme Court in the line of succession."
Several vice presidents have had to take on the presidency after the president has died or resigned.

answer only "The president can influence the federal judicial process in several ways, including

Group of answer choices

ruling on the constitutionality of congressional actions and signing treaties.

appointing federal judges and issuing pardons and amnesties.

appointing federal judges and ruling on the constitutionality of Congressional actions.

signing treaties and issuing pardons and amnesties."
appointing federal judges and issuing pardons and amnesties.

answer only "Which statement about trends in popularity levels among presidential administrations is accurate?

Group of answer choices

Virtually all presidents leave office more popular than when they entered.

Virtually all presidents leave office less popular than when they entered.

Virtually all presidents have about the same level of popularity when they take office as when they leave office.

There is no consistent trend; the differences between administrations does not reveal any particular pattern."
Virtually all presidents leave office less popular than when they entered.

answer only "Powers of the presidency not explicitly spelled out in the Constitution nor given to the executive branch through acts of Congress are best described as which of these?

Group of answer choices

explicit powers

delegated powers

inherent powers

usurped powers"
inherent powers

answer only "In the last few decades, executive orders have

Group of answer choices

become increasingly rare due to the Congress imposing limits on them.

become focused almost exclusively on emergency wartime measures rather than on domestic policy matters.

become routine instruments of presidential governance rather than emergency wartime measures.

ended entirely due to a series of Supreme Court decisions outlawing most forms of unilateral presidential action."
become routine instruments of presidential governance rather than emergency wartime measures.

answer only "Which Act requires the president to submit a budget to Congress?

Group of answer choices

Budget and Accounting Act

Congressional Impoundment and Control Act

Balanced Budget Act

Gramm-Rudman-Hollings Act"
Budget and Accounting Act

answer only "Which of the following statements about Congress and the executive branch is MOST accurate?

Group of answer choices

The Constitution explicitly prohibits Congress from providing specific guidelines to executive agencies for implementing laws.

Since around the time of the Civil War, Congress has tended to draft legislation that offers very specific guidelines for implementation by the executive.

Congress has never given executive agencies broad mandates and has always drafted legislation that offers very specific guidelines for implementation by the executive.

Starting around the time of the New Deal, Congress has tended to draft legislation that offers few clear guidelines for implementation by the executive."
Starting around the time of the New Deal, Congress has tended to draft legislation that offers few clear guidelines for implementation by the executive.

answer only "The complex structure of offices, tasks, rules, and principles that organize all large-scale institutions to coordinate the work of their personnel is known as bureaucracy.

Group of answer choices

True

False"
True
